name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 2026 European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) is a one‑week program in Vienna, Austria, aimed at PhD students, young researchers and senior scholars working in AI. It offers introductory and advanced courses covering all AI sub‑disciplines, with special thematic tracks on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, and includes a series of invited tutorials and networking events. The school also provides a rich social programme, including a reception at Vienna City Hall, and is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and the University of Oslo and takes place at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries and doctoral students, and focuses on multilingual large language models, their evaluation, data curation, and dialectal challenges. The program combines technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and field activities such as a bus transfer and mountain‑side setting, and participation is subsidized with no registration fee but participants cover travel and accommodation costs.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment featuring state-of-the-art expertise in machine learning and artificial intelligence. The school welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals to participate.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of topics in computer vision, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers participants the chance to upskill in the latest AI theory and applications through three specialised tracks, taught by world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry. The programme is flexible, allowing attendees to choose any combination of tracks and can be taken in Oxford or online. Prospective participants are invited to monitor the timeline for upcoming registration details.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026, organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden, targets doctoral and postdoctoral researchers. It covers a broad spectrum of topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making, combining keynote talks, industry panels and collaborative brainstorming sessions. The six‑day event, held at the Happy Tammsvik resort, provides fully funded accommodation, meals and activities to foster new research collaborations.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh that brings together master’s‑level students and industry practitioners to explore robotics. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and hands‑on sessions covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications. Participants also benefit from networking opportunities, industry talks and a special dinner, with support from the National Robotarium and the UK‑RAS network.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a short‑paper presentation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems brings together researchers and practitioners to discuss interdisciplinary challenges of AI and AS. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session, and a social program to foster networking and professional exchange. The event takes place at Umeå University in Sweden from 11 to 13 March 2026, with accommodation options and a registration fee structure for students, faculty, and others.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM’27) brings together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech processing, affective computing and human‑robot interaction. It targets PhD students and early‑career researchers, while also welcoming industry participants, and offers lectures, hands‑on sessions and informal networking in a mountain setting near Grenoble. The programme aims to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange, bridge theory and practice, and stimulate new collaborations at the intersection of social AI, HRI and multimedia.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference includes a Winter School held from 11 to 15 January 2027. It focuses on recent advances in deep learning such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction, and speech recognition, targeting researchers and practitioners. The event takes place at UiT The Arctic University of Norway in Tromsø, providing opportunities for networking and collaboration.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals from Nepal and abroad. The school brings together over 500 in‑person participants and thousands of online viewers to explore fundamentals and cutting‑edge AI research.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers a series of lectures and tutorials delivered by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, and the University of Cambridge, covering topics in machine learning and artificial intelligence. The program is aimed at graduate students and early‑career researchers seeking intensive training and networking opportunities in the field.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day intensive program featuring keynotes, lectures and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, focusing on topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. The school emphasizes diversity, networking, and opportunities to present research through poster sessions while fostering collaboration between academia, industry and the broader Mediterranean community.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program jointly organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre. It targets master and Ph.D. students, post‑docs, young researchers, and professionals, offering expert lectures, hands‑on labs, and networking opportunities to develop cutting‑edge AI skills. The school takes place at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco, and aims to build the next generation of AI leaders in the region.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing (AthNLP) Summer School 2026 takes place at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program offers intensive lectures and hands‑on sessions covering modern NLP methods, targeting graduate students and researchers in the field. It is jointly organized by several research institutes and universities, and it coincides with the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in nearby Chania.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It is designed for STEM students with intermediate experience in AI/ML who want theoretical knowledge and practical skills for real‑world applications. The programme is offered as a residential or online course, providing hands‑on experience and career‑oriented training.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars in Caen, Normandy, France. It covers AI techniques for scientific data analysis, experimental design, generative models, and integration with physical simulations through hands‑on workshops and case studies. The program aims to equip participants with deep‑learning skills and practical Python experience to accelerate scientific discovery across disciplines.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and inventive thinking that combine business and technology topics. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university who wish to gain 3 ECTS credits while experiencing Finnish summer life and participating in workshops, group work and social activities. The programme includes both on‑site teaching at the Lappeenranta campus and optional online components, with additional cultural events such as sauna evenings and nature excursions.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the inaugural International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, held in Porto–Maia, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026. The program targets graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium, and open‑debate sessions without formal academic prerequisites. Participants can attend face‑to‑face or live online, receive recordings for 45 days after the event, and earn a certificate of participation.
